seoTitle: onsemi Products Distributor | IGBT, MOSFET, IPM, Sensors | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse onsemi product categories: IGBT modules, MOSFETs, SiC devices, intelligent power modules, sensors. In stock, competitive pricing, technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by onsemi?
faqs.answer: onsemi offers five core product categories: (1) IGBT Modules - High-performance modules including FGY series for motor drives and renewable energy; (2) MOSFETs - Power MOSFETs including SiC and silicon solutions for efficient power conversion; (3) Intelligent Power Modules (IPMs) - Integrated modules with gate drivers and protection for motor control; (4) Power Management - Comprehensive PMICs for automotive and industrial applications; (5) Sensors - Advanced sensing solutions for automotive, industrial, and IoT applications. Each category offers multiple series optimized for specific applications and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: How do I choose between IGBT modules and MOSFETs for my application?
faqs.answer: The choice between IGBT modules and MOSFETs depends on your application requirements: Choose IGBT modules for high-voltage (600V+), high-current applications such as industrial motor drives above 5kW, solar inverters, wind power converters, and EV charging stations. IGBTs offer lower conduction losses at high currents and better cost-performance for high-power applications. Choose MOSFETs for lower voltage applications (below 600V), high-frequency switching, and applications requiring fast switching speeds such as DC-DC converters, synchronous rectification, and low-voltage motor drives. For the highest efficiency at high voltages, consider onsemi's SiC MOSFETs which combine the best of both technologies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our IGBT modules or MOSFETs categories to compare specifications and find the optimal solution for your power requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of onsemi's intelligent power modules (IPMs)?
faqs.answer: onsemi's intelligent power modules (IPMs) offer significant advantages for motor control applications: (1) Integration - IPMs integrate IGBTs or MOSFETs with gate drivers, protection circuits, and bootstrap diodes in a single package; (2) Simplified Design - Reduced component count and PCB complexity compared to discrete solutions; (3) Built-in Protection - Over-current, short-circuit, under-voltage lockout, and over-temperature protection; (4) Optimized Performance - Gate drivers matched to power devices for optimal switching; (5) Faster Time-to-Market - Pre-validated solution reduces design and testing time. IPMs are ideal for applications such as HVAC compressors, industrial motor drives, appliance motor control, and pump/fan applications where reliability and fast development are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our Intelligent Power Modules category to find integrated solutions for your motor control applications.

faqs.question: Which onsemi sensor type is best for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: onsemi offers multiple sensor technologies for automotive applications: (1) Image Sensors - High-resolution CMOS image sensors for ADAS, surround view, and autonomous driving systems; (2) Hall Effect Sensors - Non-contact magnetic sensing for position, speed, and current measurement in motor control and battery management; (3) Pressure Sensors - MEMS-based pressure sensors for tire pressure monitoring (TPMS) and engine management; (4) Temperature Sensors - Precision temperature sensing for battery management and thermal management systems. For ADAS and autonomous driving applications, onsemi's image sensors provide industry-leading performance. For electric vehicle battery management, Hall sensors offer accurate current measurement with galvanic isolation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Visit the Sensors category to explore image sensors, Hall sensors, and related products for your automotive application needs.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of onsemi SiC MOSFETs over traditional silicon devices?
faqs.answer: onsemi's Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs offer significant advantages over traditional silicon MOSFETs and IGBTs: (1) Higher Efficiency - Lower switching losses enable higher switching frequencies, reducing passive component size; (2) Higher Power Density - Reduced cooling requirements and smaller magnetic components; (3) Zero Reverse Recovery - Body diode has no reverse recovery charge (Qrr=0), eliminating switching losses in hard-switching applications; (4) Higher Temperature Operation - Junction temperatures up to 175°C compared to 150°C for silicon; (5) Lower On-Resistance - Enables high-current handling with minimal conduction losses. These benefits make onsemi SiC MOSFETs ideal for solar inverters, EV charging, and industrial power supplies where efficiency and size are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our SiC MOSFETs in the MOSFETs category to find high-efficiency solutions for your power conversion applications.

faqs.question: Do I need a gate driver for onsemi IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Yes, gate drivers are essential for proper IGBT and MOSFET operation. Gate drivers provide: (1) Isolation - Galvanic isolation between control circuits and power devices; (2) Drive Capability - High peak current for fast switching to minimize losses; (3) Protection Features - Desaturation detection for short-circuit protection, under-voltage lockout (UVLO), and soft shutdown; (4) Reliability - Proper switching characteristics to protect power semiconductors from damage. Using dedicated gate drivers ensures safe, reliable operation. For onsemi IGBT modules, select gate drivers with adequate isolation voltage, peak output current of 2A or higher, and propagation delays under 200ns for precise switching control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team for gate driver recommendations compatible with your onsemi IGBT modules and MOSFETs.

faqs.question: What voltage and current ratings are available in onsemi IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: onsemi IGBT modules span a wide range of voltage and current ratings: Voltage Ratings - 600V for low-voltage industrial drives, 1200V for standard industrial and automotive applications (most common), 1700V for high-voltage drives and renewable energy. Current Ratings - From 75A in compact modules to 300A+ in high-power packages. The FGY series covers 75A-300A at 1200V, ideal for industrial motor drives. Discrete IGBTs offer 10A-100A in standard packages for induction heating and welding. These modules are designed for applications including motor drives, solar inverters, wind power converters, and EV charging systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on the IGBT Modules category to browse specific voltage and current ratings and find the right module for your power requirements.

faqs.question: Which onsemi products are suitable for electric vehicle applications?
faqs.answer: onsemi offers a comprehensive portfolio for electric vehicle applications: (1) IGBT Modules - FGY series modules for traction inverters and DC-DC converters; (2) SiC MOSFETs - High-efficiency devices for onboard chargers (OBC) and DC fast charging; (3) Intelligent Power Modules - Integrated solutions for HVAC compressors and auxiliary systems; (4) Gate Drivers - Isolated drivers for high-voltage traction systems; (5) Sensors - Image sensors for ADAS, Hall sensors for current measurement in motor control and battery monitoring. All automotive products meet AEC-Q100/Q101 standards and are designed for the demanding requirements of electric vehicle systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our EV-related solutions in the Solutions category or browse automotive-qualified products in each product category.

faqs.question: What support does BeiLuo provide for onsemi product selection?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive support for onsemi product selection: (1) Technical Consultation - Our FAE team can help identify the optimal onsemi components for your specific application; (2) Cross-Reference Service - We can suggest onsemi alternatives for obsolete or competitor parts; (3) Sample Support - Evaluation samples available for qualified projects; (4) Design Resources - Access to datasheets, application notes, reference designs, and simulation models; (5) BOM Optimization - Recommendations for cost reduction and performance improvement; (6) Long-term Supply Planning - Forecasting and allocation support for high-volume production. Our team has deep expertise across all onsemi product lines and applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for personalized assistance with onsemi product selection and design optimization.

faqs.question: Are onsemi products available from stock or made-to-order?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics maintains extensive inventory of popular onsemi products for immediate shipment: In-Stock Items - Common IGBT modules (FGY75T120SWD, FGY100T120SWD), popular MOSFETs, standard IPMs, and frequently used sensors are typically available from stock with same-day or next-day shipping. Made-to-Order Items - Specialized configurations and custom-packaged devices may require lead times of 8-16 weeks. We also offer bonded inventory programs for high-volume customers, ensuring dedicated stock allocation for your production needs. Contact us for current stock status and lead time information.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check individual product pages for stock status, or contact sales for lead time confirmation and volume pricing.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: High-performance IGBT modules for power conversion, motor control, and renewable energy applications. Industry-leading efficiency and reliability.
categories.longDescription:
onsemi is a leading provider of IGBT (Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor) module technology, offering comprehensive solutions for industrial, automotive, and renewable energy applications. onsemi's IGBT modules combine high power density, low switching losses, and exceptional reliability to enable efficient power conversion in demanding applications.

The FGY series IGBT modules are designed for industrial motor drives and general purpose inverters, available in current ratings from 75A to 300A and voltage ratings of 600V and 1200V. These modules feature advanced trench-gate field-stop technology for optimal switching performance and low conduction losses.

Key technology features include low-inductance module designs, integrated temperature sensing, and robust packaging for reliable operation in harsh environments. All modules are qualified according to industry standards and manufactured in ISO-certified facilities. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBT Modules selection guidance and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select the Right onsemi IGBT Module for Your Project
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal onsemi IGBT module based on voltage, current, and application requirements

faqs.question: How do I read the IGBT module parameter table for proper selection?
faqs.answer: Understanding IGBT module parameters is crucial for proper selection: Collector-Emitter Voltage (Vce) - Must exceed your DC bus voltage with safety margin (typically 1.5-2x); Collector Current (Ic) - Choose based on RMS current with 1.5-2x derating for reliability; Vce(sat) - Lower values mean lower conduction losses but may trade off switching speed; Switching Energy (Eon/Eoff) - Determines switching losses at your operating frequency; Package Type - Standard packages for compatibility; Temperature Range - Industrial (-40°C to +150°C) or extended ratings. Our product tables show all these parameters with typical values at 25°C. For detailed characterization curves, refer to the datasheet or contact our FAE team for application-specific guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any specific model in the table to view detailed specifications, or contact us for selection assistance based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 600V and 1200V IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Voltage rating selection depends on your DC bus voltage and safety margins: 600V IGBTs - For 230-400VAC applications with DC bus up to 400V, typical in small motor drives, pumps, and HVAC systems; 1200V IGBTs - For 380-480VAC industrial applications with DC bus up to 600-700V, most common for general-purpose drives and inverters. Safety Margin - Select Vce rating at least 1.5-2x your maximum DC bus voltage to account for voltage spikes and transients. Overvoltage spikes during switching can exceed DC bus voltage by 20-30%, so adequate voltage margin is essential for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Filter the table by voltage rating using the dropdown above, or contact our technical team for voltage selection guidance based on your specific application.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power losses for IGBT module selection?
faqs.answer: Total IGBT power losses consist of conduction losses and switching losses: Conduction Losses = Vce(sat) × Ic × Duty Cycle. Use the Vce(sat) value from the table at your operating current and junction temperature. Switching Losses = (Eon + Eoff) × Switching Frequency. Eon and Eoff values are provided in the datasheet at specific test conditions. Total Losses = Conduction Losses + Switching Losses. Thermal Considerations - Multiply total losses by thermal resistance (Rth) to calculate temperature rise. Ensure junction temperature stays below maximum rating (typically 150°C) with adequate margin. For high-frequency applications (>10kHz), switching losses dominate; for low-frequency/high-current applications, conduction losses are more significant. Our FAE team can provide detailed loss calculations for your specific operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select an IGBT module from the table with adequate current rating and low Vce(sat) for your efficiency requirements, or request a detailed loss analysis from our technical team.

faqs.question: What thermal management considerations apply to IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Proper thermal management is critical for IGBT reliability: Thermal Resistance (Rth) - Listed in the table as Rth(j-c) junction-to-case and Rth(c-s) case-to-heatsink; calculate total thermal resistance for your cooling system. Maximum Junction Temperature - Typically 150°C; operate below 125°C for long-term reliability. Heat Sink Selection - Size based on total power losses and maximum allowable temperature rise. Thermal Interface Material - Use high-quality thermal grease or phase-change materials between module and heatsink. Mounting Torque - Follow specified torque values for screws to ensure proper thermal contact without damaging the module. Forced Air vs. Liquid Cooling - High-power modules (>200A) typically require liquid cooling or substantial heatsinks with forced air. Our FAE team can assist with thermal calculations and cooling system design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review thermal specifications in the table, then click on specific models for detailed thermal data and mounting guidelines.

faqs.question: Can I parallel IGBT modules for higher current capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, IGBT modules can be paralleled for higher current, but careful design is required: Current Sharing - Use modules with positive temperature coefficient (most modern IGBTs) so current naturally balances as devices heat up. Gate Drive - Use identical gate drivers with matched propagation delays; connect gates through individual resistors to prevent oscillation. Symmetrical Layout - Ensure equal trace lengths and impedances for all parallel devices to balance switching. Derating - Parallel 20-30% more devices than theoretically required to account for current imbalance. Temperature Sensing - Monitor each module individually as they may operate at different temperatures. onsemi FGY series modules are designed for parallel operation with matched characteristics. Contact our FAE team for detailed parallel connection guidelines and design review.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select IGBT modules from the table with appropriate current ratings for your parallel configuration, or contact us for parallel design assistance.

products.longDescription:
The onsemi FGY75T120SWD is a high-performance IGBT from the FGY series, designed for industrial motor drives, inverters, and power supply applications. This device features onsemi's advanced trench-gate field-stop technology, delivering low conduction losses and optimized switching performance.

Rated at 1200V collector-emitter voltage and 75A continuous collector current, the FGY75T120SWD is ideal for single-phase and small three-phase inverter applications. The TO-247-3 package is an industry-standard form factor ensuring compatibility with existing designs and wide availability of compatible heat sinks.

Key features include fast switching characteristics, low saturation voltage, and robust operation across the full temperature range. The device is qualified according to industry standards and suitable for demanding industrial environments.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my 10 years supporting industrial drive customers, the FGY75T120SWD has consistently been a reliable choice for small to medium power applications. What stands out is its excellent balance between conduction and switching losses at an attractive price point. I've successfully used this device in numerous motor drive designs, and it performs reliably even in harsh industrial environments. The fast switching characteristics help reduce EMI, which is often a concern in compact designs. For applications requiring 5-15kW output power, this IGBT offers an optimal cost-performance ratio.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent cost-performance ratio with balanced conduction and switching losses

faqs.question: What applications is the FGY75T120SWD best suited for?
faqs.answer: The FGY75T120SWD is optimized for medium-power industrial applications: Industrial Motor Drives - Single-phase and small three-phase inverters for AC motors up to 7.5kW; Power Supplies - Switch-mode power supplies and DC-DC converters; General Purpose Inverters - Pump and fan control, conveyor systems, HVAC applications; Renewable Energy - Small solar string inverters. Key application requirements that match this device: 380-480VAC input voltage, output power 3-7.5kW, switching frequency 5-20kHz, continuous collector current up to 50A RMS. The 1200V rating provides adequate margin for 600V DC bus applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: This device is ideal for your small to medium power industrial drive application. Contact us for volume pricing and delivery schedules.

faqs.question: How does the FGY75T120SWD compare to competitor 75A IGBTs?
faqs.answer: The FGY75T120SWD offers competitive advantages: Low Vce(sat) - 1.85V typical provides good efficiency; Fast Switching - Optimized for 5-20kHz switching frequencies common in motor drives; Industry-Standard Package - TO-247-3 form factor ensures compatibility; Cost-Effective - Competitive pricing for general-purpose applications; Reliable Performance - Qualified for industrial temperature range. Compared to premium competitors, it offers similar performance at a more attractive price point. For specific competitor comparisons, contact our FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: The FGY75T120SWD offers excellent performance and value for industrial applications. Request a sample to evaluate in your design.

faqs.question: What are the key installation considerations for the FGY75T120SWD?
faqs.answer: Proper installation ensures optimal performance and reliability: Thermal Interface - Apply thin uniform layer of thermal grease between device and heatsink; Mounting Torque - Tighten mounting screw to specified torque (typically 0.8-1.2 Nm); Electrical Connections - Ensure proper torque on terminals to prevent overheating; Gate Drive - Use recommended gate resistor values (typically 10-47Ω) to control switching speed; Layout - Minimize stray inductance in power connections to reduce voltage overshoot; ESD Protection - Handle with proper ESD precautions. For detailed mounting instructions, download the application note from the datasheet section.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download the datasheet and application notes for detailed mounting guidelines, or contact our technical support for installation assistance.

faqs.question: How can I verify the authenticity of my FGY75T120SWD device?
faqs.answer: Verify genuine onsemi devices with these checks: Packaging - Authentic devices ship in onsemi-branded packaging with proper labeling; Markings - Clear laser markings with part number, date code, and country of origin; Physical Inspection - High-quality molding, consistent color, no visible defects; Documentation - Genuine products include traceability documentation; Source - Purchase only from authorized distributors like BeiLuo Electronics. Red Flags - Misspelled markings, poor print quality, unusual packaging, prices significantly below market rate. BeiLuo Electronics guarantees 100% authentic onsemi products with full traceability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Order from BeiLuo Electronics to ensure genuine onsemi products with full documentation and warranty support.

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate driver for the FGY75T120SWD?
faqs.answer: The recommended gate driver for FGY75T120SWD is the onsemi NCP51820: Specifications - 4A peak output current for fast switching, high-side and low-side outputs, integrated dead-time control; Features - Under-voltage lockout (UVLO), shoot-through protection, wide operating voltage range; Package - SOIC-8 for compact designs; Performance - Fast propagation delay enables precise switching control. Gate Resistor Selection - Typically 10-47Ω depending on desired switching speed and EMI requirements. Lower values = faster switching but higher overshoot. The NCP51820 is available from stock at BeiLuo Electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add the NCP51820 gate driver to your order, or contact us for complete inverter design support including gate drive circuits.

faqs.question: How does the FGY100T120SWD compare to the FGY75T120SWD?
faqs.answer: The FGY100T120SWD offers 33% higher current capability compared to FGY75T120SWD: Current Rating - 100A vs 75A continuous collector current; Vce(sat) - Slightly higher at 1.90V vs 1.85V due to larger die size; Package - Identical TO-247-3 package for easy upgrade; Switching Energy - Proportionally higher due to larger die; Thermal Performance - Similar thermal resistance but higher power handling. The FGY100T120SWD is the natural upgrade path when your application requires more power while maintaining the same PCB footprint and design approach.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FGY100T120SWD when you need higher current capability in the same package, or contact us for application guidance.

faqs.question: What are the thermal design considerations for the FGY100T120SWD?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for FGY100T120SWD requires careful consideration: Power Dissipation - Higher current means higher conduction losses; calculate based on your RMS current and duty cycle. Heat Sink Selection - Larger heat sink required compared to 75A devices; consider forced air cooling for continuous high-current operation. Thermal Interface - Use high-quality thermal grease for optimal heat transfer. Mounting - Ensure proper mounting torque and flatness for good thermal contact. Temperature Monitoring - Consider implementing temperature-based current derating for maximum reliability. Our FAE team can provide thermal modeling and heat sink selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal analysis and heat sink recommendations specific to your operating conditions.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements does the FGY100T120SWD have?
faqs.answer: The FGY100T120SWD has similar gate drive requirements to other IGBTs: Gate Voltage - +15V recommended for full enhancement, -8V to -15V for negative off-state voltage; Gate Charge - Higher than 75A devices due to larger die size; Gate Resistor - Typically 10-47Ω depending on switching speed requirements; Gate Driver Current - Minimum 2A peak recommended for fast switching; Isolation - Use isolated gate drivers for high-voltage applications. The NCP51820 is well-suited for this device with its 4A output capability. For parallel operation, use individual gate resistors for each device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use the NCP51820 gate driver or contact us for gate drive circuit design recommendations.

faqs.question: Can the FGY100T120SWD be used in parallel configuration?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FGY100T120SWD can be paralleled for higher current capability: Current Sharing - Use devices from same production batch for best matching; Gate Drive - Use identical gate drivers with matched propagation delays; Layout - Symmetrical layout with equal trace lengths is critical; Gate Resistors - Use individual gate resistors (5-10Ω) to prevent oscillation; Temperature Sensing - Monitor each device individually. Parallel Configuration - Common in high-power motor drives requiring 200A+ capability. Two FGY100T120SWD devices in parallel can handle up to 180-200A with proper derating. Contact our FAE team for detailed parallel design guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for parallel configuration design review and recommendations.

The NTHL series power MOSFETs feature low on-resistance and fast switching characteristics, making them ideal for DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power supply applications. onsemi's SiC MOSFETs offer breakthrough performance with zero reverse recovery, enabling higher switching frequencies and improved system efficiency.

Key technology features include advanced trench-gate structures for low RDS(on), optimized cell designs for fast switching, and robust packaging options including TO-220, TO-247, and surface-mount packages. All devices are qualified to industry standards with AEC-Q101 automotive qualification available. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive MOSFET selection guidance and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select the Right onsemi MOSFET for Your Project
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal onsemi MOSFET based on voltage, current, and application requirements

faqs.question: How do I select the right voltage rating for a MOSFET?
faqs.answer: MOSFET voltage rating selection depends on your application requirements: For DC-DC converters and low-voltage motor drives (12V-48V systems), select 60V-100V MOSFETs with adequate margin; For 110V-240VAC applications, select 500V-650V MOSFETs to handle rectified DC bus voltages up to 400V; For high-voltage industrial applications, select 800V-1200V devices. Safety Margin - Choose VDS rating at least 1.3-1.5x your maximum operating voltage to account for voltage spikes and transients. Consider avalanche energy rating for inductive load applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our MOSFET categories by voltage rating, or contact our technical team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: What is the significance of RDS(on) in MOSFET selection?
faqs.answer: RDS(on) is the drain-source on-resistance and directly impacts conduction losses: Conduction Loss = I² × RDS(on) × Duty Cycle. Lower RDS(on) means lower conduction losses and higher efficiency. Trade-offs - Lower RDS(on) typically means larger die size, higher gate charge, and higher cost. Selection Guidelines - For high-current applications (>10A), prioritize low RDS(on); For high-frequency switching (>100kHz), balance RDS(on) with gate charge; For cost-sensitive applications, select the lowest RDS(on) that meets thermal requirements. Temperature Effect - RDS(on) increases with temperature (typically 1.5x at 100°C), so consider worst-case conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare RDS(on) specifications in our MOSFET tables to find the optimal balance for your application.

faqs.question: When should I choose SiC MOSFETs over silicon MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Choose SiC MOSFETs when your application benefits from: Higher Efficiency - 30-50% lower switching losses enable higher switching frequencies; Higher Power Density - Smaller magnetic components and reduced cooling requirements; Higher Temperature Operation - Junction temperatures up to 175°C vs 150°C for silicon; Zero Reverse Recovery - Eliminates switching losses in hard-switching bridge topologies; Higher Voltage Applications - Superior performance at 800V+ compared to silicon superjunction devices. Cost Consideration - SiC MOSFETs have higher initial cost but can reduce overall system cost through smaller passives and cooling systems. Ideal Applications - EV charging, solar inverters, data center power, high-frequency industrial drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our SiC MOSFET offerings or contact us for SiC vs silicon comparison for your specific application.

products.description: 650V superjunction power MOSFET with low on-resistance
products.longDescription:
The onsemi NTHL040N65S3F is a 650V superjunction power MOSFET from the NTHL series, designed for high-efficiency power conversion applications. This device features advanced superjunction technology, delivering low on-resistance of 40mΩ and fast switching characteristics.

Rated at 650V drain-source voltage and 40A continuous drain current, the NTHL040N65S3F is ideal for power supplies, motor drives, and inverter applications operating from 110-240VAC mains. The TO-220F package provides excellent thermal performance with isolated mounting tab.

Key features include low gate charge for fast switching, low RDS(on) for minimal conduction losses, and robust avalanche capability. The device is qualified according to industry standards and suitable for demanding industrial and consumer applications.

descriptionParagraphs: The onsemi NTHL040N65S3F is a 650V superjunction power MOSFET featuring advanced technology.
With 40mΩ on-resistance and 40A current capability, this device delivers excellent efficiency.
The TO-220F package provides isolated mounting and excellent thermal performance.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The NTHL040N65S3F is an excellent choice for 500W-1kW power supply designs. The 40mΩ RDS(on) provides good efficiency without excessive gate charge, making it suitable for both hard-switching and soft-switching topologies. I've used this device in numerous PFC and LLC designs with excellent results. The TO-220F package is particularly useful when isolation between the MOSFET and heat sink is required. For designers looking for a reliable 650V MOSFET in the 40A class, this device offers competitive performance at an attractive price point.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent balance of RDS(on) and gate charge for 500W-1kW power supplies

faqs.question: What applications is the NTHL040N65S3F best suited for?
faqs.answer: The NTHL040N65S3F is optimized for high-efficiency power conversion: Switch-Mode Power Supplies - PFC stages, LLC resonant converters, flyback converters for 500W-1kW; Motor Drives - Low-voltage motor control, BLDC motor drives; Solar Inverters - Microinverters and string inverters; LED Drivers - High-power LED lighting; DC-DC Converters - Industrial and telecom power systems. Key specifications matching these applications: 650V rating for 400V DC bus, 40A current for moderate power levels, 40mΩ RDS(on) for good efficiency, low gate charge for fast switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: This MOSFET is ideal for your 500W-1kW power supply or motor drive application. Contact us for volume pricing.

faqs.question: How does the NTHL040N65S3F compare to standard planar MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: The NTHL040N65S3F superjunction technology offers significant advantages: Lower RDS(on) - 40mΩ vs 80-120mΩ for comparable planar devices; Faster Switching - Lower gate charge enables higher switching frequencies; Higher Efficiency - Reduced conduction and switching losses; Smaller Die Size - Superjunction technology achieves lower RDS(on) in smaller area. Trade-offs - Superjunction devices have higher output capacitance (Coss) which can affect light-load efficiency in some topologies. For hard-switching applications above 50kHz, the superjunction advantages typically outweigh any disadvantages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose the NTHL040N65S3F for high-efficiency applications where superjunction benefits justify the cost premium over planar MOSFETs.

faqs.question: What are the key layout considerations for the NTHL040N65S3F?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout ensures optimal performance: Gate Drive Loop - Keep gate traces short and wide to minimize inductance; use Kelvin source connection for high-current applications. Power Loop - Minimize loop area between drain and source connections to reduce EMI; use copper planes for high-current paths. Thermal Design - Ensure adequate copper area for heat spreading; use thermal vias under the drain tab. Decoupling - Place bypass capacitors close to the device for stable gate drive. Gate Resistor - Position close to the MOSFET gate pin to dampen ringing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our layout guidelines or contact our FAE team for PCB design review.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for the NTHL040N65S3F?
faqs.answer: The NTHL040N65S3F performs optimally with: Recommended VGS - +10V to +12V for full enhancement and lowest RDS(on); Minimum VGS - +8V to ensure adequate conduction; Negative VGS - -2V to -5V for fast turn-off and improved noise immunity; Gate Resistor - 5-22Ω depending on switching speed requirements. Higher gate voltage (12V) provides lowest RDS(on) but increases gate drive losses. For most applications, 10V provides the best balance. The device is fully characterized at VGS=10V in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10-12V gate drive voltage for optimal performance, or contact us for gate drive circuit recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power losses for the NTHL040N65S3F?
faqs.answer: Total power losses consist of conduction and switching losses: Conduction Loss = I² × RDS(on) × Duty Cycle. Use RDS(on) at operating temperature (typically 1.5x room temperature value). Switching Loss = 0.5 × VDS × ID × (tr + tf) × fsw + Eoss × fsw. Use switching times from datasheet at your gate drive conditions. Total Loss = Conduction Loss + Switching Loss. For thermal design, ensure junction temperature stays below 125°C for long-term reliability. Our FAE team can provide detailed loss calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power loss calculations and thermal design recommendations.

products.description: 650V superjunction power MOSFET with ultra-low on-resistance
products.longDescription:
The onsemi NTHL025N65S3F is a 650V superjunction power MOSFET featuring ultra-low on-resistance of 25mΩ. This device is designed for high-efficiency applications where minimizing conduction losses is critical.

With 650V voltage rating and 47A current capability, the NTHL025N65S3F is ideal for high-power power supplies, motor drives, and renewable energy applications. The TO-220F package provides isolated mounting for safety-critical applications.

Key features include exceptionally low RDS(on) for minimal conduction losses, robust avalanche capability, and fast switching characteristics. The device is qualified to industry standards and suitable for demanding industrial applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The NTHL025N65S3F is my top recommendation when efficiency is the primary concern. The 25mΩ RDS(on) provides exceptional conduction loss performance, making it ideal for high-current applications where every watt counts. I've used this device in server power supplies and solar inverter designs where efficiency directly impacts operating costs. While the gate charge is higher than lower-RDS devices, the conduction loss savings more than compensate in high-current applications. For 1kW+ power supplies and motor drives, this MOSFET delivers outstanding performance.
faeReview.highlight: Exceptional low RDS(on) for maximum efficiency in high-current applications

faqs.question: What makes the NTHL025N65S3F ideal for high-efficiency designs?
faqs.answer: The NTHL025N65S3F excels in high-efficiency applications due to: Ultra-low RDS(on) - 25mΩ minimizes conduction losses, especially at high currents; Optimized Cell Design - Superjunction technology provides low RDS(on) without excessive die size; Fast Switching - Good switching characteristics for high-frequency operation; Low Qg - Despite low RDS(on), gate charge is optimized for reasonable drive requirements. Efficiency Impact - In a 1kW power supply at full load, the 25mΩ device can save 10-15W compared to 40mΩ alternatives, directly improving system efficiency and reducing cooling requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose NTHL025N65S3F when efficiency is critical and the application justifies the premium over standard RDS(on) devices.

faqs.question: How does the NTHL025N65S3F compare to the NTHL040N65S3F?
faqs.answer: The NTHL025N65S3F offers significant RDS(on) improvement: RDS(on) - 25mΩ vs 40mΩ (37% lower conduction losses); Current Rating - 47A vs 40A continuous; Gate Charge - 55nC vs 35nC (higher due to larger die); Package - Both use TO-220F; Cost - Premium pricing for lower RDS(on). Selection Guide - Choose NTHL025N65S3F for highest efficiency in high-current applications; Choose NTHL040N65S3F for cost-sensitive designs where 40mΩ is acceptable. The efficiency improvement of the 25mΩ device typically pays for itself in reduced operating costs in high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare total cost of ownership including efficiency gains when selecting between these devices.

faqs.question: What thermal considerations apply to the NTHL025N65S3F?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for NTHL025N65S3F: Power Dissipation - Calculate based on your RMS current and RDS(on) at operating temperature; Conduction Loss = I² × 25mΩ × 1.5 (temp coefficient). Heat Sink Requirements - Larger heat sink than 40mΩ devices due to higher current capability; consider forced air for continuous high-current operation. Thermal Interface - Use high-quality thermal grease; ensure flat mounting surface. Temperature Derating - RDS(on) increases with temperature; maintain Tj < 125°C for reliability. The device can handle higher currents but requires adequate cooling to maintain safe junction temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal analysis and heat sink selection for your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from the NTHL025N65S3F?
faqs.answer: The NTHL025N65S3F is ideal for: High-Power PFC - Active power factor correction stages in 1kW+ supplies; Server Power Supplies - Where efficiency directly impacts operating costs; Solar Inverters - String inverters requiring high efficiency; Motor Drives - High-current BLDC and induction motor drives; EV Charging - Onboard chargers and DC-DC converters; Telecom Power - Rectifiers and DC-DC converters. Applications where the 37% lower RDS(on) provides meaningful efficiency improvement and cost savings over the product lifetime justify the premium pricing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate total cost of ownership including energy savings when considering the NTHL025N65S3F for your design.

faqs.question: What gate drive considerations apply to the NTHL025N65S3F?
faqs.answer: Gate drive requirements for NTHL025N65S3F: Gate Voltage - +10V to +12V recommended for lowest RDS(on); Gate Charge - 55nC requires higher drive capability than lower-current devices; Gate Resistor - 5-15Ω typical, adjust for switching speed vs EMI; Driver Current - Minimum 2A peak recommended for fast switching; Switching Speed - Can achieve <50ns switching with proper drive. The higher gate charge means slightly higher gate drive losses, but this is negligible compared to conduction loss savings. Use a robust gate driver like NCP51820 for best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NCP51820 gate driver or equivalent with 2A+ capability for optimal switching performance.
